title: Support consecutive repetition [*N] in SVA properties
body:
## Summary

Add support for the SVA consecutive repetition operator `[*N]`
(IEEE 1800-2023 Section 16.9.2) in property expressions. The operator is
lowered in V3AssertPre to an equivalent conjunction of `$past()` checks:
`a[*3]` becomes `a && $past(a,1) && $past(a,2)`.


## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  logic clk, a, b;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) a[*3] |-> b);
endmodule
```

On master:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: ...: Unsupported: [*] boolean abbrev expression
```

## Changes

- **`src/V3AstNodeExpr.h`**: New `AstConsRep` node representing `expr[*N]`
  with two children: the expression and a constant count.
- **`src/verilog.y`**: Grammar rule for `expr [*N]` creates `AstConsRep`
  instead of parser-level expansion. Removed `[*N]` exact-count case from
  `boolean_abbrev` to avoid grammar conflict.
- **`src/V3Width.cpp`**: Width visitor for `AstConsRep` -- validates count
  is constant and positive, sets bit dtype.
- **`src/V3AssertPre.cpp`**: Lowering pass expands `AstConsRep(expr, N)` to
  `expr && $past(expr,1) && ... && $past(expr,N-1)` with proper clocking.

Range `[*M:N]`, unbounded `[*]`/`[+]`, and other repetition types remain
unsupported.

## Test

- `test_regress/t/t_assert_prop_consec_rep.{v,py}` -- functional tests for
  `[*1]`, `[*2]`, `[*3]` with overlapped `|->` and non-overlapped `|=>`
  implications. Uses CRC random stimulus.
- `test_regress/t/t_assert_prop_consec_rep_bad.{v,py,out}` -- error coverage
  for non-constant count and `[*0]` unsupported cases.
- `test_regress/t/t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` -- updated golden to remove
  error expectations for `[*N]` exact count.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
